zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Harrison於1539年出生在英國素福爾可斯普盧頓時，她的父親William Harrison25歲，母親Frances Female35歲。她與Thomas Gardner結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1601年5月11日在英國倫敦享年62歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可。

nameMeanings.name: Harrison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): from the Middle English personal name Harry or Herry + -son. These were the common pronunciations of Henry (see Harry ).

History: Harrison is an extremely common surname in northern England. One important and influential American family of bearers are descended from Benjamin Harrison, who emigrated from England to VA in 1633 or 1634. Descendants include another Benjamin Harrison (c. 1726–91), who was an activist in the American Revolution and a signer of the Declaration of Independence. His son William Henry Harrison (1773–1841) and great-grandson Benjamin Harrison (1833–1901) both became presidents of the US.

variants.name: Harry
meanings: English (mainly south Wales and southwestern England): from the medieval personal name Harry, a common form of Henry , with assimilation of -nr- to -rr- and regular Middle English change of -er- to -ar-. In south Wales the surname is generally a post-medieval coinage, in most cases not hereditary until the 18th or 19th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hariric, composed of the elements hari, heri ‘army’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Harry (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Arrizon
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): probably an altered form of English   Harrison  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Harrison was born in 1539, in Sproughton, Suffolk, England, her father, William Harrison, was 25 and her mother, Frances Female, was 35. She married Thomas Gardner. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 11 May 1601, in London, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Middlesex, England.

Elle a épousé Thomas Gardner. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 mai 1601, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Middlesex, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Harrison(이)가 1539년 영국 수폴크 스프루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Harrison(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Frances Female(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Gardner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1601년 5월 11일 영국 런던에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Gardner. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 11 de maio de 1601, em London, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Harrison родилась в 1539 г. в местности спроугтон, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Harrison было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Frances Female, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Gardner. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 11 мая 1601 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности миддлесекс, Англия.
